items.text: Have you had persistent fevers, night sweats, fatigue, or unexplained weight loss?
choices: High fevers, night sweats, and weight loss

items.text: Do you have a history of valve disease, a heart murmur, a prosthetic valve, or a cardiac device?
choices: No history of valve disease or cardiac device
Known heart murmur or valve disease
Prosthetic heart valve or implanted cardiac device

items.text: Have you used intravenous drugs, or have you had recent dental work or surgeries?
choices: No IV drug use or recent invasive procedures
Recent minor surgery or dental cleaning
Active intravenous drug use or major dental surgery/extraction

items.text: Have you had new shortness of breath, chest pain, or swelling in your legs?
choices: Mild exertional dyspnea or trace leg edema
Orthopnea, chest pain, or severe peripheral edema

items.text: Have you noticed any new painful or painless spots, bumps, or lines on your skin, fingers, or toes?
choices: Splinter hemorrhages, painful nodules on fingers, or painless red spots on palms/soles

items.text: Have you had a sudden weakness, numbness, vision changes, or severe headache?
choices: Acute focal weakness, sensory loss, vision changes, or meningeal headache

items.text: Have you noticed blood in your urine or flank pain?
choices: Gross hematuria or acute flank pain

items.text: Have you had new joint pain or back pain?
choices: Severe localized back pain or acute joint swelling

items.text: Have you taken antibiotics recently before your blood cultures were drawn?
choices: Completed antibiotic course over 2 weeks ago
Recent or active antibiotic use within past 2 weeks

items.text: Do you have an indwelling venous line, hemodialysis catheter, or a history of recurrent bloodstream infections?
choices: No indwelling catheters or recurrent infections
Active central line, hemodialysis catheter, or prior bacteremia

items.text: Auscultate the heart for a new murmur or changes to an existing murmur.
choices: No murmur or stable chronic murmur
New diastolic murmur or changing murmur severity

items.text: Assess body temperature, heart rate, blood pressure, and perfusion status.
Inspect palms and soles for painless, erythematous macular lesions (Janeway lesions).
Inspect pads of fingers and toes for painful, erythematous nodules (Osler nodes).
Inspect fingernails and toenails for linear dark red streaks (splinter hemorrhages).
Examine the conjunctivae for petechiae and perform fundoscopy to assess for retinal hemorrhages (Roth spots) if trained.
choices: Roth spots present on fundoscopic exam

items.text: Auscultate lungs for crackles, wheezing, or evidence of pulmonary congestion.
choices: Diffuse bilateral crackles indicating heart failure

items.text: Palpate the abdomen for splenomegaly or left upper quadrant tenderness.
choices: No splenomegaly or left upper quadrant tenderness
Left upper quadrant tenderness without splenomegaly
Palpable splenomegaly or severe tender splenic infarct findings

items.text: Perform a focused neurologic exam assessing strength, sensation, cranial nerves, and signs of embolic stroke.
choices: New focal neurologic deficit (hemiparesis, aphasia) suggesting embolic event

items.text: Inspect any indwelling catheter or line site for erythema, warmth, tenderness, or purulent drainage.
choices: No indwelling lines, or line site is clean and quiet
Mild erythema without drainage at line site
Erythema, purulent drainage, or tenderness at line site
